claim: Thornburg 2026's 4D whole-cell model predicts a mean/median mRNA half-life of approximately 3.63 min across all 452 protein-coding genes of JCVI-Syn3A.
source_detail: Reported as emergent 4DWCM-level property. 3.63 min = 218 s. The paper's prior (Thornburg 2022) used a ~2 min half-life extrapolated from Mycoplasma gallisepticum; the 2026 result suggests Syn3A-specific mRNA is longer-lived. Per-gene distribution of half-lives is available in the Zenodo release (10.5281/zenodo.15579158) but not extracted in Session 11.
caveats: This is a SIMULATED value from Thornburg 2026's model, not a direct experimental Syn3A measurement. The authors explicitly flag genome-wide Syn3A mRNA half-life measurement as an open experimental gap.
Per-gene variance is not captured by this single number; some mRNAs may have half-lives 10x shorter or longer.
Supersedes the implicit ~2 min value we'd have used from Thornburg 2022 if we had recorded it. Layer 1 code that treats all mRNAs with a single half-life should use this value.
Extracted from WebSearch snippets in Session 11.
